title: Seragon Publishes Peer-Reviewed Data: SRN-901 Extends Mouse Lifespan 33%, Outperforms Rapamycin
description: Seragon Biosciences published peer-reviewed data in Drug Design, Development and Therapy showing its multi-target oral drug SRN-901 extended median remaining lifespan by 33% in adult mice on a Western diet -- outperforming rapamycin (+21%), NMN, and NR in head-to-head comparison. The drug also attenuated frailty progression by 70% and reduced tumor incidence by 30.5%. Multi-omics analysis showed coordinated modulation of mTOR, autophagy, NAD+, and senolytic pathways, with metabolic profiles of treated mice resembling those of younger animals.

title: Vitamin C Identified as Direct ACSL4 Inhibitor, Reverses Biological Age in Primate Study
description: Published in Cell Metabolism, a landmark study identified 'ferro-aging' -- a conserved iron-lipid axis driving primate aging through ACSL4 enzyme activity. Through screening 100 ferroptosis-related molecules, researchers found Vitamin C directly binds and inhibits ACSL4. A 40-month intervention in aged cynomolgus monkeys reversed biological age by 3-7 years across epigenetic, transcriptomic, and metabolomic clocks, improved brain volume, reduced visceral fat, and lowered LDL cholesterol. This repositions Vitamin C from a generic antioxidant to a specific, druggable geroprotective agent.

title: Longevity Biotech Rally Continues: ARKG +7.7%, XBI +4.4%, IDNA +4.9% on Second Consecutive Up Day
description: Longevity and genomics ETFs continued their powerful rally on Wednesday, extending Tuesday's gains. ARKG led again at +7.66%, followed by IDNA +4.91%, XBI +4.44%, SBIO +4.27%, IBB +2.40%, HTEC +2.34%, and BBH +2.16%. The only decliners were defensive healthcare plays XLV (-1.27%) and IHI (-0.20%). The two-day rally has added billions in market cap to the tracked longevity sector, signaling strong risk-on sentiment in biotech and genomics.

title: Bryan Johnson Promotes Simple Balance Test as Biological Age Proxy at Business Insider Event
description: At Business Insider's 'The Long Play' event in San Francisco, Bryan Johnson demonstrated a one-leg balance test with eyes closed as a simple proxy for biological age. The approach is supported by a Mayo Clinic study finding one-legged balance time is a valid measure of frailty, independence, and fall risk. Johnson's public visibility continues to mainstream longevity concepts for general audiences.

title: IGF-1 Signaling Cannot Rescue Mitochondrial Mutator Mice: New Hierarchy in Longevity Pathways
description: A new study published in Science Advances found that reduced IGF-1 signaling -- one of the most robust longevity pathways in animal models -- fails to extend lifespan in mitochondrial mutator mice. This reveals a hierarchy where mitochondrial genome integrity is a prerequisite for IGF-1-mediated longevity benefits, potentially explaining why some longevity interventions work in some contexts but not others.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Peer-Reviewed Publication: 33% Lifespan Extension, 70% Frailty Reduction in Mice
description: Seragon Biosciences published full preclinical data in Drug Design, Development and Therapy showing SRN-901 extended median remaining lifespan by 33% in 18-month-old mice, outperforming rapamycin (+21%), NMN, and NR. Multi-omics data show coordinated modulation of mTOR, autophagy, NAD+, and senolytic pathways.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Published in Cell Metabolism, researchers identified 'ferro-aging' -- iron-driven lipid peroxidation via ACSL4 -- as a conserved primate aging mechanism. 40-month Vitamin C treatment in aged monkeys reversed biological age by 3-7 years on multi-omic clocks, improved brain volume, and enhanced metabolic health.

title: Bryan Johnson Presents One-Leg Balance Test as Biological Age Proxy at BI's 'The Long Play'
description: Bryan Johnson demonstrated a simple eyes-closed one-leg balance test as a biological age proxy at Business Insider's event in San Francisco. A Mayo Clinic study supports one-leg balance as a valid measure of frailty and fall risk in adults 50+.

title: Reduced IGF-1 Signaling Fails to Extend Lifespan in Mitochondrial Mutator Mice
description: New study reveals that reduced IGF-1 signaling cannot rescue mitochondrial mutator mice, establishing a hierarchy where mitochondrial genome integrity is required for IGF-1-mediated longevity benefits.

title: Maryland Becomes First State to Sign Longevity Ready Act Into Law
description: Governor Wes Moore signed the Longevity Ready Maryland Act (HB 278/SB 113), making Maryland the first state to enact legislation specifically addressing the impact of longevity with a 10-year multisector plan.

title: Homoharringtonine Validated as Novel Senolytic: FDA-Approved Drug Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Researchers screened 2,150 compounds and identified HHT as a potent senolytic that clears senescent cells via HSPA5-mediated apoptosis, reverses metabolic dysfunction, and extends lifespan in progeroid and aged mice.

title: HMGB1 Box A Gene Therapy Reverses Aging Biomarkers in Monkey Study
description: IV administration of HMGB1 Box A plasmid in perimenopausal monkeys reversed age-related proteomic changes, improved bone health, reduced blood glucose, and decreased weight gain over 56 weeks with no adverse effects.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026, Projected $8-9B Full Year
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events totaling $3.74B, a 56% increase over Q1 2025. DLT analysis projects $8-9B for full-year 2026, confirming the sector's transition from thesis to mainstream investment opportunity.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D for First Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, raised $80M to advance ER-100, an AAV-delivered OSK gene therapy for optic neuropathies, through the first-ever human trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming.

title: David Sinclair Teases April 2026 Launch for Longevity Research Funding Initiative
description: Harvard professor David Sinclair posted that 'longevity research is being starved of funds' and revealed he has assembled a team with a launch date in April 2026 to address the funding gap.

title: Vascular Endothelium Aging Review: Mitochondrial Metabolism as Therapeutic Target
description: New review examines how aging reshapes endothelial cell metabolism and mitochondrial function, proposing mitochondria-driven metabolic deterioration as a key therapeutic target for vascular aging.

title: Sex Differences in Aging Interventions: New Review Highlights Understudied Female Biology
description: New review examines sexually dimorphic lifespan and healthspan effects of interventions in rodents, highlighting the need to study X chromosome, gonadal hormones, and ovarian health in aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin Trial
description: Over 700 South Texans will participate in one of the largest human aging trials, testing rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin to determine if they slow biological aging. ARPA-H committed $144M to support the work.

title: Arg-1 Identified as Key Regulator of Age-Dependent Cartilage Repair via Macrophage Polarization
description: Single-cell omics revealed that Arg-1 overexpression rescues impaired cartilage repair in aged animals by restoring anti-inflammatory macrophage subsets, establishing a novel therapeutic target for age-related joint degeneration.

title: Rapamycin Review: Promising for Longevity but Standardized Human Biomarkers Still Lacking
description: Comprehensive review finds low-dose intermittent rapamycin appears safe over one year in healthy adults, but current data do not yet support it as a reliable intervention for extending human lifespan without standardized pharmacodynamic biomarkers.

title: Bryan Johnson 2026 Blueprint Stack: Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 supplement update includes NMN/NR six days per week, removal of rapamycin (stopped 2024), and addition of low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function and cellular support.

title: SASP Score From Blood Proteomics Predicts Mortality Using Deep Learning
description: New composite SASP Score from UK Biobank blood proteomics data via deep learning validates as a predictor of mortality and chronic conditions; multimodal exercise alters its trajectory.

title: RNA-Lipid Nanoparticle Therapy Enables Heart Self-Repair via ANP Production
description: Columbia researchers developed RNA-lipid nanoparticles encoding Nppa that produce pro-ANP in skeletal muscle, converted to ANP by Corin in the heart, reducing scarring and improving cardiac function in animal models.

title: High-Dose Influenza Vaccine Associated With Lower Alzheimer's Risk in 65+ Population
description: Study of 120,775 patients found high-dose inactivated influenza vaccine was associated with lower Alzheimer's risk in months 1-25 post-vaccination compared to standard-dose in adults 65+.

title: Senolytics Pipeline 2026: Field Splits Into Academic, Biotech, and Local Delivery Lanes
description: Comprehensive review finds human proof-of-biology exists for senolytics, but the field is splitting into academic first-gen, biotech pipeline, and local delivery approaches. Nature Aging compared 21 senolytic agents.

title: UC Berkeley Organ-on-a-Chip Platform Replicates 40 Years of Human Aging in Four Days
description: UC Berkeley researchers unveiled a miniaturized organ-on-a-chip platform that replicates 40 years of human aging in four days using aged human serum on stem-cell-derived tissues, enabling rapid screening of longevity interventions.

title: America's Longevity Crisis: Fortune Analyzes the 12.4-Year Healthspan Gap
description: Fortune reports that with the oldest boomers turning 80 in 2026, the US faces a 12.4-year gap between lifespan and healthspan. Only 5% of homes have basic accessibility features and 80% of older households lack resources for long-term care.

title: Longevity Sector Valued at $23.2B in 2026, Projected $34.8B by 2030
description: The longevity biotech sector stands at $23.2B in 2026 and is projected to reach $34.82B by 2030 at an 11% CAGR, driven by record Q1 funding and expanding clinical pipelines.

title: Longevity Center Europe: Clients Aging 22% Slower Than Average on DunedinPACE
description: Over 80 individuals at Longevity Center Europe tested with DunedinPACE scored an average 0.78, meaning they are aging 22% slower than the general population, with at least 10 clients aging slower than Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference Opens April 20 in Atlanta: Largest US Aging Event
description: The ASA's On Aging 2026 conference takes place April 20-23 at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta, themed 'The Power of Belonging' and focusing on amplifying voices and advancing justice in aging.
